calendar.matters:
Motion For Order Sealing The Record [C.R.C. 2.550; 2.551] *** Redacted Version ***
Off Calendar Sep-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David K. Ries, Esq. (415-554-4212; david.ries@sfcityatty.org) Deputy City Attorney for The Health Officer for The City and County of San Francisco.
Kara Chien, Esq. (415-553-9308; kara.chien@sfgov.org) of the Office of the Public Defender for John Doe.

Discussed. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER JOHN DOE'S Motion For Order Sealing The Record [C.R.C. 2.550; 2.551].

Petitioners' unopposed motion for an order sealing certain records containing medical and protected health information is granted. The motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
